channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: News Corp Australia explains crawler bot access issues
item.description: The message appears to be an automated response from News Corp Australia's website, explaining that the site uses software to manage crawler bot traffic. It outlines steps users should take if they're...

item.title: India Accelerates Bullet Train Project with Indigenous Tech
item.description: India is fast-tracking its high-speed railway project by reducing reliance on Japanese expertise and embracing indigenous technology. The Mumbai-Ahmedabad high-speed rail corridor, which began constru...

item.title: Cybersecurity Needs Integration Over Tool-Based Strategies
item.description: The article argues that relying solely on cybersecurity tools like Managed Detection and Response (MDR) and Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) is insufficient. Cybercriminals prioritize finding wea...

item.title: FIFA Faces Challenges in Addressing Wildfire Smoke During World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final, set between Argentina and Spain near New York City, faces potential health risks from wildfire smoke originating in Canada. Air quality indices have reached 'unhealthy' ...

item.title: Kenyan Engineering Graduate Shares Post-Graduation Struggles and Career Advice
item.description: A Kenyan engineering graduate has shared her honest experiences two years after graduating, highlighting challenges like credential inflation, corporate disillusionment, and family dynamics. She revea...

item.title: Lebanese President Meets Trump in Washington to Discuss Ceasefire and Regional Security
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has left Beirut for Washington, where he will meet Donald Trump. This marks the first visit by a Lebanese head of state to the US capital since 2009. The trip follows su...

item.title: Scottish Nationalism and Historical Imperialism: A Reevaluation
item.description: The article critiques Scottish nationalism's claims of oppression by highlighting Scotland's historical role as a pillar of British imperialism. Author Geoff Russ argues that Scotland was never an opp...

item.title: 11 scholars from BARMM lawmaker's office graduate with high honors
item.description: Eleven college scholars from poor Moro families, supported by the office of Bangsamoro regional lawmaker Sha Elijah Dumama-Alba, graduated with high honors from various universities in the autonomous ...

item.title: Technical access issue prevents reading Melbourne weather forecast
item.description: The article details a technical barrier preventing users from accessing Melbourne's weather forecast. News Corp Australia's website employs software to manage crawler bot traffic, resulting in a block...

item.title: Laos Investigates Methanol-Tainted Alcohol Linked to Six Tourist Deaths
item.description: Laos has stated it cannot determine the cause of six tourist deaths linked to methanol-tainted alcohol in November 2024. The victims, including a Briton, two Australians, two Danes, and an American, d...

item.title: Philippine Eagle Dies After Rehabilitation
item.description: The Philippine eagle Sawaga-Dalwangan, rescued from a troop of monkeys in Bukidnon, died during rehabilitation at the Philippine Eagle Center. The PEF confirmed her death after sudden seizures on Satu...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates with Attacks on Critical Infrastructure
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified, with both nations launching attacks that have targeted critical infrastructure in the Middle East. On July 18, 2026, the U.S. military ...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua Celebrates Ol Kalou Victory with Remarks on Election Tactics
item.description: Rigathi Gachagua, former deputy president, celebrated his Ol Kalou by-election victory with a speech that highlighted his campaign strategies and criticized President William Ruto's tactics. During th...

item.title: Canada's economic strategy under Carney faces criticism for lack of progress
item.description: Conrad Black's article critiques Prime Minister Mark Carney's failed efforts to reduce Canada's economic dependence on the United States. Despite public pledges to diversify trade relationships, the U...

item.title: Ethekwini Municipality Faces Backlog of Unpaid Invoices for Water and Sanitation Contractors
item.description: Over 60 water and sanitation contractors under the eThekwini Municipality have been demanding payment for outstanding invoices, with some claiming they are owed tens of thousands of rands. The contrac...

item.title: Call for Transparent Inquiry into Minister's Controversies
item.description: The article emphasizes the need for an independent, impartial investigation into allegations involving a senior public official. It highlights that due process and presumption of innocence are fundame...

item.title: Astronomers Discover Sun-like Star That Ate One of Its Planets
item.description: Researchers have found strong evidence that TOI-5882, a Sun-like star approximately 1,300 light-years from Earth, may have consumed one of its planets. The discovery was made by analyzing the star's c...

item.title: President William Ruto's Website Restored After Cyberattack Attempt
item.description: President William Ruto's official website was hacked on Saturday, July 18, 2026, with attackers defacing the homepage and demanding a Bitcoin ransom of KSh 41 million. The site was temporarily taken o...

item.title: Thomas Tuchel's coaching decisions under scrutiny in England's World Cup semi-final defeat
item.description: The article critically examines Thomas Tuchel's tactical approach during England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina, arguing that his safety-first strategy stifled the team's potential. It highl...

item.title: Election Commission extends Maharashtra voter roll revision timeline to August 8 due to heavy rainfall
item.description: The Election Commission has extended the deadline for the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Maharashtra till August 8 after the state's Chief Electoral Officer sought additional t...
